authority_note: This record documents product-strategy decisions and evidence for continuity. It does not authorize product implementation, repository changes outside the LAB repository, release, integration, deployment, or code execution.

source_context.origin: Conversation analysis of Waypoint investor concept and subsequent Claude/Perplexity audits.
source_artifacts: Waypoint investor PDF: tracking de grupo en tiempo real, sin instalacion
Auditoria critica v1: contradictions and MVP reduction
Auditoria v2: RISER/Cardo, REVER, Convoy Tracker, Detecht and tourism B2B analysis

current_thesis.summary: Waypoint should be treated as a B2B-oriented operations platform for guides, agencies, tourism operators and mixed travel groups, not as a motorcycle-first consumer convoy app.
current_thesis.positioning: Plan routes on web, execute in mobile app, monitor operations in real time.
current_thesis.primary_surface: Web planner and lightweight live operations view for operators.
current_thesis.field_surface: Android-first mobile app for guides, drivers, staff or tracked operational units.
current_thesis.guest_surface: Web link or QR for tourists/viewers with itinerary, meeting point, help action and optional check-in. Guests are not primary tracked units in the MVP.

decisions.title: Native mobile app required for reliable tracked units
decisions.summary: Any unit expected to share reliable movement/location during a trip should use the mobile app. Browser/web users should be treated as viewers, planners, admins or low-reliability participants, not as core tracked units.
decisions.rationale: Mobile browser background geolocation is not reliable enough for the product promise. The previous no-install tracking thesis is superseded.

decisions.title: Web is for planning and operations, not core mobile tracking
decisions.summary: The web version is valuable for route planning, checkpoint organization, link/QR generation, agency workflow, and monitoring one or more active groups from a larger screen.
decisions.rationale: This resolves the web-vs-native contradiction and strengthens the B2B agency/tourism use case.

decisions.title: Motorcycle-first positioning is deprioritized
decisions.summary: Do not use motorcycle clubs as the primary beachhead until RISER/Cardo Ride, REVER, Detecht and Convoy Tracker are tested in the field and clear gaps are verified.
decisions.rationale: The motorcycle niche already has specialized competitors, including hardware/community incumbents. Motos can remain a secondary or benchmark segment.

decisions.title: Primary validation shifts to guides, agencies and tourism operations
decisions.summary: Initial validation should focus on guides, small agencies, tourism adventure, mixed vehicle groups and operators who currently depend on WhatsApp, radios and manual protocols.
decisions.rationale: Existing tour-operator software appears stronger in bookings, payments and itineraries than in live group tracking and operational monitoring.

decisions.title: MVP scope becomes web planner/live view plus Android field app
decisions.summary: The MVP should be reduced to one minimal web surface with plan/live views, one Android-first field app for guides/staff/tracked units, and a web guest link. iOS, advanced operations, safety automation, audio, traffic, weather, ice, replay and multi-agency dashboards remain out of MVP.
decisions.rationale: This avoids building three full products before validation. Android-first is aligned with LATAM/Chile market share and field-device reality.

decisions.title: Safety automation and crash detection remain out of commercial MVP
decisions.summary: The MVP may include manual help/report actions, last known location, stopped/no-signal states and basic operational alerts. Automatic accident detection, sensor fusion and precise road-ice claims remain research/shadow-mode only.
decisions.rationale: Safety claims create legal and reputational risk without field data, labeled datasets and validation.

web: Show passive state: active, stopped, late, no signal, arrived
android_app: Queue state updates when connectivity degrades

actions: Interview 3-5 guides or agencies with concrete pricing in hand
Validate whether monitoring guides/vehicles/staff is enough without tracking every tourist
Ask whether they would pay USD 29 per guide or USD 79 per small agency per month
Document current WhatsApp/radio/manual failures with timestamps from real tours
Benchmark RISER/Cardo Ride, REVER, Detecht and Convoy Tracker before re-opening motorcycle-first positioning
Estimate map and location infrastructure cost per active tour
validation_plan.b2b_success_metric: Guide or agency uses Waypoint-like workflow in three or more tours within 30 days and expresses willingness to pay.
validation_plan.consumer_metric_deprioritized: Second trip in 60 days remains useful for B2C, but it is not the primary MVP kill metric.

commercial_hypothesis.agency_basic: USD 79/month for up to three guides or active groups
commercial_hypothesis.agency_pro_later: USD 149/month for larger operations after multi-group dashboard exists
commercial_hypothesis.pricing_status: Hypothesis for interview validation, not approved pricing.

open_questions: Will agencies accept tracking only guides/vehicles/staff instead of every tourist?
Is one live trip view enough for the first paying pilot?
What is the real willingness to pay for small agencies in LATAM?
What map provider keeps cost per tour acceptable?
Can low-power Android tracking stay reliable for 4-8 hour tours?
Which legal disclaimer is required for manual help/SOS actions?
